races.title: Reno Mayor
races.notes: Open seat — incumbent Mayor Hillary Schieve is termed out. 9 candidates on the June 9, 2026 primary ballot; top two advance to the Nov. 3, 2026 general. Nonpartisan, open to ALL voters. Dominant issues: structural budget deficit (~$24-26M) and a hot-button data center moratorium.

races.title: Reno City Council Ward 2
races.notes: Open seat — incumbent Naomi Duerr is termed out. 4 candidates in the June 9, 2026 primary; top two advance to November. Redrawn Ward 2 covers west Reno from West Fourth Street to Lakeridge Golf Course. Nonpartisan, open to ALL voters.

races.title: Reno City Council Ward 4
races.notes: Only 2 candidates filed (incumbent Meghan Ebert and Eddie/Eduardo Gomez), so there is NO June primary for this seat — both advance directly to the Nov. 3, 2026 general. Ward 4 covers the North Valleys and the largest area zoned for industrial/data center development. Included here for completeness.

candidates.name: Devon Reese
candidates.bio: Reno-raised attorney who has served on the Reno City Council since 2019 (appointed At-Large, elected At-Large in 2020, then elected to the Ward 5 seat when the At-Large seat was eliminated).

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KOLO Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates
This Is Reno — Kate Marshall endorsed over data center stance (Reese reversal detailed)
KUNR — Reno mayoral candidates outline challenges and solutions
Wikipedia — 2026 Reno mayoral election

youtubeInterviews.title: Know Your Candidates: Meet Devon Reese, candidate for Reno Mayor
Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

dataCenters.stance: Reversed course in spring 2026 to back a data center moratorium as a 'pause' for guardrails, after previously opposing regulation/moratorium as recently as Feb. 2026.

water.stance: Campaign centers on a large Truckee River park; would relocate the Waste Management transfer station to protect the city's drinking water source.

growth.stance: Pitches a unifying 'greater vision' for development along the Truckee River across political and economic lines.
growth.quote: Whether you're a Republican or a Democrat, whether you're rich or poor...you can see a greater vision.

candidates.name: Kate Marshall
candidates.bio: Former federal prosecutor, two-term Nevada State Treasurer (elected 2006), and Nevada Lieutenant Governor (2019-2021) who later served as a senior advisor in the Biden White House.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KUNR — Reno mayoral candidates outline challenges and solutions
This Is Reno — Kate Marshall endorsed for Reno Mayor over data center stance
Wikipedia — 2026 Reno mayoral election

youtubeInterviews.title: Know Your Candidates: Meet Kate Marshall, candidate for Reno Mayor
Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

housing.stance: Calls housing the city's biggest challenge and criticizes unplanned 'build, baby, build' growth that strains rather than strengthens neighborhoods.
housing.quote: Housing is personal to me. Growing up, every time the rent went up, we moved.

dataCenters.stance: Consistently opposed since January 2026 — data centers do not belong within Reno city limits; favors regional siting parameters.
dataCenters.quote: I don't think data centers belong in Reno city limits, full stop.

budget.stance: Frames fiscal management as a top priority, citing her record managing state budgets and investments during the recession as Treasurer.

candidates.name: Kathleen Taylor
candidates.bio: Native Nevadan small business owner and former Reno Planning Commission member; appointed to the Ward 1 (downtown) City Council seat in 2022 and elected in 2024, currently serving as vice-mayor. Endorsed by Gov. Joe Lombardo.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KUNR — Reno mayoral candidates outline challenges and solutions
This Is Reno — data center debate (Taylor's two anti-moratorium votes)
Mike's Reno Report — What Lombardo's endorsement of Kathleen Taylor reveals

youtubeInterviews.title: Know Your Candidates: Meet Kathleen Taylor, candidate for Reno Mayor
Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

budget.stance: Makes fiscal discipline her central message, noting the city's recurring multimillion-dollar deficit and the need to say no to spending.
budget.quote: Every decision that we make has got to begin with fiscal discipline...saying no sometimes.

dataCenters.stance: The only council member to vote against the data center moratorium; opposes a moratorium as bad for business, though now says she supports regulating data centers.
dataCenters.quote: I don't support a moratorium...It's bad for business in Reno.

housing.stance: Supports boosting housing supply by lessening or loosening barriers to permitting.

candidates.name: George "Eddie" Lorton
candidates.bio: Self-funded small business owner and perennial citywide candidate making his fourth bid for Reno mayor (also ran 2014, 2018, 2022, finishing runner-up to Schieve in 2018 and 2022). Endorsed by the Washoe County Republican Party.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KUNR — Reno mayoral candidates outline challenges and solutions
Mike's Reno Report — Lombardo endorsement / GOP split
Wikipedia — 2026 Reno mayoral election

youtubeInterviews.title: Know Your Candidates: Meet Eddie Lorton, candidate for Reno Mayor
Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

budget.stance: Would sell city-owned properties to pay down debt and avoid continual utility/fee tax increases.
budget.quote: I'd be selling off these city-owned properties so that we could pay down the debt.

dataCenters.stance: Supports the moratorium, especially keeping data centers out of the downtown core.
dataCenters.quote: That's a no-brainer to me...I don't think they should be in the downtown core.

housing.stance: Would require that 20% of approved developments be affordable housing.
housing.quote: I have solutions for affordable housing...we won't approve them unless 20 percent are affordable housing.

transparency.stance: Promises independence from developer and casino interests as a self-funded candidate; emphasizes transparency at City Hall.

candidates.name: Tim Ross
candidates.bio: Reno High and UNR alumnus, former small business owner and substitute teacher, retired Washoe County Sheriff's Office sergeant, and former Nevada PERS board member; runs as unaffiliated.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KUNR — Reno mayoral candidates outline challenges and solutions
Wikipedia — 2026 Reno mayoral election

youtubeInterviews.title: Know Your Candidates: Meet Tim Ross, candidate for Reno Mayor
Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

budget.stance: Calls out structural and cultural dysfunction in city finances and inconsistent messaging about whether the budget is balanced; wants audits.
budget.quote: The city feels like it's just floating right now. It needs some leadership...structural...cultural...dysfunction.

dataCenters.stance: Skeptical of data centers as big users of water and power whose costs fall on residents.
dataCenters.quote: They take up nice property...big users of resources, water and power...costs come down to us.

publicSafety.stance: Frames his core mission as keeping Reno safe, clean and financially sound, drawing on his law enforcement background.

candidates.name: Corinthia Yancey
candidates.bio: Wisconsin native who moved to Reno in 2018; small business owner running a security company with private and government contracts and a member of the Washoe County Board of Equalization.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KUNR — Reno mayoral candidates outline challenges and solutions
KOLO Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

youtubeInterviews.title: Know Your Candidates: Meet Corinthia Yancey, candidate for Reno Mayor
Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

growth.stance: Wants Reno to grow but not at the expense of water resources or rising utility bills; favors reasonable developer standards over a 'tech rush.'
growth.quote: I want Reno to grow, but not at the expense of...our water...and bills rising.

dataCenters.stance: Conditional support for a moratorium 'to a certain extent,' emphasizing a strategic, accountable approach.

publicSafety.stance: Centers her campaign on strengthening public safety, supporting small businesses, and increasing accountability in leadership.

budget.stance: Says the city budget needs a redefined structure to better distribute resources.
budget.quote: The budget, it needs more of a redefining structure that can help facilitate proper distribution.

candidates.name: Jesse Razo
candidates.bio: Longtime Reno resident who says he has been 'building Reno for the past 25 years'; previously ran for Reno mayor in 2018 and 2022.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KOLO Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

youtubeInterviews.title: Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

growth.stance: Raises concerns about population growth; pitches himself as having no outside funding, personal agenda, or conflicting investments.

candidates.name: John Wayne Zink
candidates.bio: Musician and entrepreneur who founded the IT Avalon consulting firm and is known as 'The Mayor' on the YouTube channel 'Casino Carisa and The Mayor'; moved to Reno about four years ago.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KOLO Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

youtubeInterviews.title: Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

budget.stance: Identifies city overspending as the primary challenge; focus on business and community.

candidates.name: Greg "Nutt" Nuttle
candidates.bio: Downtown Reno resident and bartender whose name remains on the June 9 ballot, though he announced in late May 2026 that he was wrapping up his campaign, saying he would not make it past the primary.

sources.label: mynews4 Know Your Candidates — Reno Mayor roundup
KOLO Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates
Wikipedia — 2026 Reno mayoral election

youtubeInterviews.title: Reno Mayor Race: Meet the Candidates

growth.stance: Priorities were developing downtown and addressing congestion and growth (campaign since suspended).

candidates.name: Matt Johnson
candidates.bio: Ward 2 council candidate with government/service experience through AmeriCorps and a Conservation Corps; runs a grassroots, door-knocking campaign.

sources.label: Mike's Reno Report — Reno Ward 2: Three Campaigns, Three Strategies
AOL/RGJ — Johnson, Luetkehans, Pellett, Vaupel running in Reno Ward 2

growth.stance: Authored an opinion piece titled 'A failure to plan,' emphasizing planning and neighborhood engagement over consultant-driven campaigning.

candidates.name: Summer Pellett
candidates.bio: COO of Pellett Construction LLC and the Ward 2 fundraising leader, backed heavily by construction, development, and trade interests (AGC PAC, Sierra Nevada Construction, Laborers Local 169).

sources.label: Mike's Reno Report — Reno Ward 2: Three Campaigns, Three Strategies
AOL/RGJ — Johnson, Luetkehans, Pellett, Vaupel running in Reno Ward 2

candidates.name: Vanessa Vaupel
candidates.bio: Former Chief of Pharmacy for the Veterans Affairs health system, running a professionalized Ward 2 campaign without PAC or major-donor backing.

sources.label: Mike's Reno Report — Reno Ward 2: Three Campaigns, Three Strategies
This Is Reno — Vaupel: Reno deserves a pause on data centers (opinion)
AOL/RGJ — Johnson, Luetkehans, Pellett, Vaupel running in Reno Ward 2

dataCenters.stance: Argues Reno deserves a deliberate 'pause' on data centers with real guardrails rather than a free-for-all (authored an op-ed making this case).

candidates.name: Ian Luetkehans
candidates.bio: Ward 2 City Council candidate; as of the late-stage campaign reporting he had not raised money or launched a website, running a minimal campaign.

sources.label: Mike's Reno Report — Reno Ward 2: Three Campaigns, Three Strategies
AOL/RGJ — Johnson, Luetkehans, Pellett, Vaupel running in Reno Ward 2

candidates.name: Meghan Ebert
candidates.bio: Incumbent Reno City Council member for Ward 4, which covers the North Valleys and the city's largest industrial-zoned area; seeking re-election in 2026.

sources.label: City of Reno — Ward 4 Meghan Ebert
Nevada Current — Reno City Council extends data center moratorium

dataCenters.stance: Supports the data center pause but insists any moratorium deadline be tied to adopting comprehensive regulations so protections are codified, not a political stunt.
dataCenters.quote: I need to have something in code, and I need to show my community that they will be protected, and that this isn't a political stunt.

candidates.name: Eddie Gomez
candidates.bio: North Valleys resident of more than 20 years and restaurateur who opened a restaurant with his father at age 17; challenging the Ward 4 incumbent on infrastructure, parks, and small business. (Also listed as Eduardo Gomez.)

sources.label: KOLO — Gomez announces bid for Reno City Council
Vote Eddie Gomez campaign site

growth.stance: Says North Valleys growth has outpaced infrastructure and services; wants more attention to infrastructure and well-maintained neighborhoods.

housing.stance: Believes Ward 4 needs greater focus on affordable housing alongside support for small businesses.
